chunk_summary: This section focuses on zinc and trace element supplementation for preterm, low birth weight (LBW), and extremely low birth weight (ELBW) infants post-discharge, emphasizing micronutrient support and lab monitoring to prevent deficiencies and manage risks like diarrhoeal disease.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of zinc and trace elements in post-discharge nutrition for preterm/LBW/ELBW infants.
Identify indications for supplementation and monitoring strategies to prevent micronutrient deficiencies.
Learn the connection between zinc supplementation and diarrhoeal disease prevention in vulnerable populations.
Review guidelines for lab monitoring of trace elements post-discharge.
key_facts: Zinc supplementation is critical for immune function and diarrhoeal disease prevention in preterm/LBW/ELBW infants.
Trace element deficiencies, including zinc, are common in preterm infants due to limited stores at birth.
WHO guidelines recommend zinc supplementation in cases of diarrhoeal disease to reduce severity and duration.
Lab monitoring post-discharge should include serum zinc levels and other trace elements to ensure adequate nutrition.
Micronutrient support is essential for growth and neurodevelopment in preterm infants.
reference_quote: WHO guidelines emphasize zinc supplementation as a key intervention for managing diarrhoeal disease in vulnerable populations, including preterm and LBW infants. (Source: https://www.who.int/newsroom/fact-sheets/detail/diarrhoeal-disease)
document_key_topics: Trace element deficiencies in preterm infants
